stimulus.content: In 1767, the Townshend Acts placed taxes on glass, lead, paint, paper, and tea shipped to the colonies. Many colonists responded by refusing to buy British goods. They called this action a boycott.

stem: How did colonists use boycotts to fight against British economic policies?

choices.text: They stopped buying British goods to hurt British trade
They asked Parliament to lower prices on all goods
They sent extra payments to British merchants at sea
They traded only with France to punish the British

answer_explanation: Colonists used boycotts by refusing to purchase British goods. This hurt British merchants and manufacturers financially, putting economic pressure on Parliament to repeal the unpopular taxes. Boycotts were a key form of colonial protest against British taxation.
